Momentum and ROC together capture price acceleration; requiring both to be positive (or negative) and increasing filters out weak or random moves. Adding an ATR volatility filter ensures the strategy only trades when the market has enough movement, while ATR-based SL/TP scales risk to current volatility, reducing overtrading in low‑volatility periods.

Combining a short‑term rate of change (ROC) with a trend confirmation from a 20‑period EMA reduces entry noise while ATR‑scaled stops and targets adapt to market volatility, thereby limiting large drawdowns that plagued the previous momentum‑only scheme.

Using momentum and ROC together captures the acceleration of price movement, while filtering with a 20‑period EMA ensures the signal aligns with the prevailing trend. This combination reduces whipsaws in ranging periods and focuses on genuine trend continuations, and ATR‑based risk limits adapt to volatility, improving risk‑adjusted returns.
